popular_professors.ranking_basis: RateMyProfessors entries sorted by rating_count desc, then average_rating desc, then would_take_again_percent desc.
popular_professors.source_summary: Matched RateMyProfessors school 'Georgetown University' (legacyId=355) and ranked all professors with at least one rating.

successful_alumni.ranking_basis: Curated from official Georgetown Advancement and alumni story pages, prioritizing person-specific profiles with explicit class-year evidence and substantive post-graduation impact described on official Georgetown domains.
successful_alumni.source_summary: Extracted from official Georgetown Advancement story pages on today.advancement.georgetown.edu.

items.name: Lori Wilson
items.bio: Georgetown says Lori Wilson, MD (C’88, M’94) is professor of surgery, associate dean of faculty development and diversity, and chief of the Division of Surgical Oncology at Howard University.
items.description: Surgical oncologist, professor, and academic medical leader.
items.evidence_quality_note: Official Georgetown story page with explicit undergraduate and medical-school class-year evidence in the title and a substantive career summary in the article body.

items.name: Christopher Rutledge
items.bio: Georgetown says Christopher Rutledge (SFS’89, Parent’23) has spent more than 25 years in the nonprofit sector, working in refugee resettlement, criminal justice reform, and homelessness advocacy.
items.evidence_quality_note: Official Georgetown Hoya Highlight page with explicit class-year evidence in the title and a substantive impact summary in the article body.

items.name: Tyrone Turner
items.bio: Georgetown says Tyrone Turner (C’86, G’89) is currently a visuals editor and photojournalist at WAMU and also contributes to NPR, building a career around human-centered storytelling and social justice.
items.description: Photojournalist, visuals editor, and NPR contributor.
items.evidence_quality_note: Official Georgetown Hoya Highlight page with explicit undergraduate and graduate class-year evidence and a substantive career summary.

items.name: Linda Schakel
items.bio: Georgetown says Linda Schakel (L’86) became a retired partner at Ballard Spahr LLP after years specializing in tax policy and tax-exempt financing and also served in the U.S. Treasury’s Office of Tax Policy.
items.description: Attorney, former Ballard Spahr partner, and tax-policy specialist.
items.evidence_quality_note: Official Georgetown alumni story with explicit class-year evidence in the title and a substantive professional summary in the article body.

items.name: Harrison Beacher
items.bio: Georgetown says Harrison Beacher (C’08) is co-owner of Coalition Properties Group, identified in the story as the top Black-owned brokerage firm in the nation according to the National Association of Real Estate Brokers.
items.evidence_quality_note: Official Georgetown alumni story with explicit class-year evidence in the title and a clear role summary in the article body.

items.name: Bidtah Becker
items.bio: Georgetown says Bidtah Becker (SFS’93) serves as chief legal counsel for the Navajo Nation Office of the President and Vice President and is a go-to expert on natural resources and water issues.
items.description: Chief legal counsel to the Navajo Nation leadership.
items.evidence_quality_note: Official Georgetown Hoya Highlight page with explicit class-year evidence in the title and a substantive professional-role summary in the article body.

items.name: Eric Ji Sun Wu
items.bio: Georgetown says Eric Ji Sun Wu (SFS’17) is co-founder and CEO of Sobo Foods and previously co-founded Gainful, linking his Georgetown STIA studies to food sustainability and entrepreneurship.
items.evidence_quality_note: Official Georgetown Hoya Highlight page with explicit class-year evidence in the title and a substantive entrepreneurship summary in the article body.

items.name: Mary Byrne Welch
items.bio: Georgetown says Mary Byrne Welch (NHS’97, G’99) dedicated her nursing career to women facing breast and gynecologic cancers and later shifted toward cancer prevention and obesity-related risk reduction.
items.evidence_quality_note: Official Georgetown alumni story with explicit undergraduate and graduate class-year evidence in the title/body and a substantive clinical-care summary.

items.name: Luisa Santos
items.bio: Georgetown says Luisa Santos (C’14) serves on the Miami-Dade County Public Schools District 9 Board, started Lulu’s Ice Cream as an entrepreneur, and later moved into public service focused on educational equity.
items.description: Public official, entrepreneur, and education-equity advocate.
items.evidence_quality_note: Official Georgetown alumni story with explicit class-year evidence in the title and a substantive public-service summary in the article body.

items.name: Ebou Cham
items.bio: Georgetown says Ebou Cham (G’14) is the interim chief certified registered nurse anesthetist at Harborview Medical Center in Seattle, balancing administrative and clinical responsibilities at a major Level 1 trauma center.
items.description: Nurse anesthesia leader and interim chief CRNA.
items.evidence_quality_note: Official Georgetown Health Magazine profile with explicit class-year evidence in the title/body and a substantive role summary.

warnings: Georgetown alumni depth was manually improved because the generic pipeline only produced 7 alumni items.
This override uses official Georgetown Advancement and alumni story pages with explicit class-year evidence in titles and/or article body for stronger person-specific coverage.
